text.title: Referral Program Operations: Fraud, Metrics, Agent Workflow
text.summary: Fraud prevention, key performance metrics, and AI-agent gotchas for operating a referral program.

rule.statement: Use deterministic fraud rules (IP overlap, payment-method match, signup velocity) rather than LLM judgment; flag candidates for human review rather than auto-banning.
rule.rationale: LLMs are not reliable fraud adjudicators. Deterministic feature computation is auditable and consistent; final ban decisions require a human-in-loop to avoid legal exposure.

list.item: Require email verification before reward eligibility
Waiting period: reward releases only after referee's first real action (purchase, N-day retention)
Flag users with >5 invites in 24h or shared payment method as fraud candidates
Minimum purchase requirement prevents disposable account abuse

section.title: Key Metrics

list.item: Participation rate = referrers / total customers; target 10-15%
Referral rate = referrals per referrer; target 2-5
Conversion rate = signups / invite clicks; target 10-25%
K-factor = invites/user × conversion; target varies by product type
CAC via referral = program cost / referred customers; target <50% of paid CAC

section.reference: Python helper to compute K from referral event data

list.item: Email 1: Program announcement with unique link
Email 2: Success story (customer who earned rewards)
Email 3: Reminder with current reward status
Email 4: Limited-time bonus (double rewards) — use sparingly

antipattern.description: Agent A/B-tests reward amounts without finance approval.
antipattern.reason: Easy to blow burn budget. Reward sizing must be constrained to a max_reward_pct_of_CAC parameter passed into every agent prompt.

antipattern.description: Reward fulfillment is not idempotent — API retries double-credit referrers.
antipattern.reason: Use a unique (referrer_id, referee_id, event) key to make credit operations idempotent. Non-idempotent fulfillment destroys trust and creates accounting errors.
Static programs decay as best advocates exhaust their networks. Rotate or refresh every 6-9 months; introduce new trigger points or temporary bonus periods.
